footer.transparencyText: Full transparency on every dollar. Reports published quarterly once donations begin.

realImpact.label: Real Impact
realImpact.title: The joke is fictional. The impact is not.
realImpact.description: Every certificate sold funds verified ocean and shark conservation. We publish where the money goes.
realImpact.forSharksText: Every sale funds verified ocean and shark conservation organizations. No vague promises, no mystery fees. Business certificates donate 70%.
realImpact.forHumanText: The rest keeps the servers running and the founder fed. Think of it as funding the Alliance's land-based operations department (staff: one very tired person).
realImpact.transparencyNote: Full transparency on every dollar. Reports published quarterly once donations begin. If a shark could read a spreadsheet, they would approve.
realImpact.legalNote: The Shark Human Alliance is a fictional entertainment brand. Conservation donations are real and verifiable.
realImpact.partner1Desc: UK-based charity dedicated to safeguarding the future of sharks through science, education and influence.
realImpact.partner2Desc: The largest international organization focused solely on ocean conservation.
realImpact.partner3Desc: Direct funding for shark research, protection initiatives and policy change worldwide.
realImpact.partner4Desc: Protecting marine fauna and one of the world's most important shark sanctuaries in Colombia.
realImpact.partnersDisclaimer: Listed as planned partners. Formal partnerships will be announced as donations begin.

items.question: Is the alliance real?
items.answer: The alliance is fictional and intentionally humorous. The conservation donations are real and verifiable.

items.question: Does membership protect me from sharks?
items.answer: No. It offers no marine protection whatsoever. It does, however, help protect actual sharks from humans, which is the statistically more relevant problem.

items.question: Where does the money actually go?
items.answer: Each certificate contributes to a conservation allocation we track and report publicly. As donations are made, we publish the recipient organizations and the amounts allocated.

items.question: Why sharks?
items.answer: Because sharks have a PR problem they did not create. Humans kill roughly 100 million sharks a year. Sharks kill very few humans. The imbalance is embarrassing.

items.question: Will I receive a certificate?
items.answer: Yes. A personalized digital certificate with your name, membership tier, and an air of surprisingly official diplomatic recognition. Delivered instantly.

items.question: Can I give this as a gift?
items.answer: Absolutely. Protected Friend Status is the most popular gift option. People frame it, share it, and nobody can explain why it works so well.

valueHook.label: Why this exists
valueHook.title: A joke for humans. A better deal for sharks.
valueHook.point1Text: Personalized, printable, and polished enough to make people ask follow-up questions.
valueHook.point2Text: Every certificate helps fund shark conservation. No vague promises. No “raising awareness” as a business model.
valueHook.point3Text: Added to the Diplomatic Registry, the exclusive list of humans who paid money to be told they are not food.
valueHook.impactLine: Every sale funds conservation through Shark Trust, Oceana, Saving the Blue, and Fundación Malpelo.

faqPage.label: FAQ
faqPage.title: Reasonable questions. Excellent answers.
faqPage.subtitle: For sensible humans who want to know what they’re buying, where the money goes, and whether sharks can read certificates.

items.question: Is the alliance real?
items.answer: The alliance is fictional and intentionally humorous. The conservation funding is real. Think of it as a comedy product with a real conservation budget behind it.

items.question: Does membership protect me from sharks?
items.answer: No. It offers no marine protection whatsoever. It does, however, help protect actual sharks from humans, which is the statistically more relevant problem.

items.question: Where does the money actually go?
items.answer: Each certificate contributes to a conservation allocation we track and report publicly. As donations are made, we publish the recipient organizations and the amounts allocated.

items.question: When do donations start, and how will they be reported?
items.answer: We publish allocations once donations are actually made. This page and our social channels will show recipient organizations and reported amounts as they happen.

items.question: Are these formal partner organizations?
items.answer: Not unless we explicitly say so. We list planned recipients first and only describe an organization as a formal partner or confirmed recipient when that is actually true.

items.question: How much does each certificate cost?
items.answer: Protected Friend is $4. Non-Snack Recognition is $19. Shark-Approved Zone is $99.

items.question: Will I receive a certificate immediately?
items.answer: Yes. You receive a personalized digital certificate. If you provide an email address, we also send the delivery link by email.

items.question: What do I receive immediately after purchase?
items.answer: A success page with your certificate preview and a download link. If you entered an email address, we also send the certificate link to that inbox.

items.question: Can I access my certificate later?
items.answer: Yes, if you provide an email address during checkout. If you skip email, the certificate is only available for immediate download after purchase and the link is not stored for later retrieval.

items.question: Can I buy this as a gift?
items.answer: Yes. You can buy a certificate as a gift and enter the recipient details during checkout. If you want reliable later access, include an email address in the purchase flow.

items.question: Why sharks?
items.answer: Because sharks have a PR problem they did not create. Humans kill roughly 100 million sharks a year. Sharks kill very few humans. The imbalance is embarrassing.

items.question: Is this a donation or a product purchase?
items.answer: It is a purchase of a humorous digital product. A conservation allocation is funded from that revenue on our side; you are not making a direct charitable donation, and it is not tax-deductible.

items.question: What size is the certificate?
items.answer: Certificates are optimized for A4 or US Letter download and printing.

faqPage.ctaText: The best way to understand the certificate is to see it. Try the live preview on our homepage — or just go for it.

membershipPage.title: Choose your level of shark diplomacy.
membershipPage.description: Three tiers of shark diplomacy, followed by three certificate styles. Choose the level first, then decide how official the paperwork should look.
membershipPage.stylesTitle: Same diplomatic status. Different levels of paperwork drama.
membershipPage.stylesDescription: Once you know which certificate tier you want, pick the design style that suits the recipient. The look changes. The diplomatic outcome does not.

styleCards.title: Luxury
styleCards.text: Ornate, polished, mildly excessive. The certificate for people who want their shark diplomacy to look alarmingly official.
styleCards.detail: Best for framed gifts, theatrical recipients, and anyone who respects a decorative border.

styleCards.title: Classic
styleCards.text: Clean, formal, impossible to embarrass. The safe choice for buyers who want the joke to land with composure.
styleCards.detail: Best for offices, sensible friends, and people who prefer their nonsense well formatted.

styleCards.title: Playful
styleCards.text: Bold, colorful, unapologetically fun. This is the version for buyers who want the certificate to arrive with immediate personality.
styleCards.detail: Best for birthdays, extroverts, and gift recipients who deserve less restraint and more shark.

membershipPage.tiersTitle: Pick the certificate tier that matches the situation.
membershipPage.tiersDescription: This is the real product choice. All three are personalized, delivered digitally, and tied to real conservation support. The difference is how much recognition, ceremony, or commercial usefulness you want attached to it.
membershipPage.protectedDescription: A polished entry point for shark lovers, beach-fearers, and suspiciously good gift buyers. Funny, personal, and surprisingly frameable.
protectedFeatures: Instant delivery for gifts and last-minute diplomacy
membershipPage.nonsnackDescription: For buyers who want stronger ceremonial energy, a better framed punchline, and official recognition of their obvious non-edibility.
membershipPage.businessDescription: For cafés, studios, offices, and institutions ready to display certified non-hostility toward sharks with complete administrative confidence.
businessFeatures: Ideal for teams, venues, and shark-adjacent prestige
membershipPage.comparisonDescription: For anyone who prefers a proper comparison before formalizing shark relations.

comparisonRows.label: Best for
comparisonRows.protected: Birthdays, personal gifts, first-time shark diplomacy

membershipPage.step1Title: Choose the right flavor of paperwork
membershipPage.step1Text: Pick the style and tier that best match the gift, the recipient, and the level of absurd official energy you are aiming for.
membershipPage.step2Title: Personalize it like you mean it
membershipPage.step2Text: Add the name, optional dedication, and gift details so it feels like an actual present rather than an administrative hallucination.
membershipPage.step3Text: The recipient gets an official-looking certificate, a registry entry, and immediate documentation of improved shark-human relations.
membershipPage.clarificationText: The certificate is a humorous gift product. It does not provide legal immunity, physical safety, anti-bite force fields, or diplomatic leverage with actual sharks. The conservation support, however, is real.
membershipPage.faqTitle: Questions a sensible buyer might still ask
membershipPage.faqDescription: A brief administrative guide for shark lovers, gift buyers, and cautious beachgoers.

questionCards.question: What is this, exactly?
questionCards.answer: A personalized digital shark certificate designed as a funny gift with real conservation support behind it.

questionCards.question: Is this a good gift for a shark lover?
questionCards.answer: Yes. It is especially strong for shark lovers, ocean people, dramatic friends, and hard-to-buy-for birthdays.

questionCards.question: Will I get something immediately?
questionCards.answer: Yes. The certificate is digital, personalized, and designed for fast delivery.

questionCards.question: Can I send it to someone else?
questionCards.answer: Yes. You can personalize it for another person and send it as a gift.

questionCards.question: Does this support real shark conservation?
questionCards.answer: Yes. The joke is fictional, but the conservation support is real.

questionCards.question: Will this actually stop a shark from eating me?
questionCards.answer: No. It will, however, improve your paperwork situation considerably.

membershipPage.joinTitle: Choose the tier. Then let the sharks review the paperwork.
membershipPage.joinText: Start with Protected Friend, step up to Non-Snack, or certify an office with Shark-Approved Zone. Then choose the style that makes the joke land best.

protectedPage.title: Protected Friend Status
protectedPage.description: Diplomatic clearance for humans who deserve a slightly warmer reception from sharks and a significantly more official-looking certificate.
protectedPage.recommended: Recommended as the core launch product for Shark Human Alliance.
protectedPage.bestUseText: This is the best tier for gifting, brand clarity and certificate-led monetization.
protectedPage.whyTitle: People frame it. People share it. Nobody can explain why.
protectedPage.whyDescription: Protected Friend Status sits exactly where the concept works best: absurd enough to start a conversation, polished enough to hang on a wall.
protectedPage.reason1Text: The name says everything. No explanation needed, no awkward unwrapping silence.
protectedPage.reason2Text: It naturally supports a personalized certificate and recognition badge.
protectedPage.reason3Text: If only one paid product exists at launch, it should probably be this one.
protectedPage.buyTitle: Next step: connect this page to checkout.
protectedPage.buyText: For now this is a polished product detail page. The next build step will be adding a purchase button, then checkout, then certificate personalization.

faqItems.question: Is this actual protection from sharks?
faqItems.answer: No. Protected Friend Status is a fictional product and does not provide real-world protection of any kind. It does fund real conservation.

faqItems.question: Why is this the main product?
faqItems.answer: Because it is the clearest entry point: one name, one certificate, instant delivery. People frame it, gift it, and talk about it.

faqItems.question: What happens after purchase?
faqItems.answer: You receive a personalized digital certificate delivered instantly to your email.

registry.title: The Diplomatic Registry
registry.subtitle: An official-ish record of humans whose shark-related paperwork is, for once, in order.
registry.description: Everyone listed here has been formally assessed and declared symbolically unsuitable for shark consumption. The designation is legally meaningless, scientifically unverified, and aesthetically excellent.
registry.loadingText: Searching the oceanic archives... Careful, some records may have been nibbled.
registry.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, referral code, or registry ID...
registry.searchNoResults: No diplomats match your search. They might be hiding from sharks.
registry.emptyState: No one is officially uneaten yet. Someone has to be first.
registry.emptyStateFounders: Founding diplomats will appear here. Be the first.
registry.viralWantedDesc: Billions of humans remain without any form of symbolic shark protection. Know someone who needs diplomatic clearance? Gift them a certificate before it's too late (symbolically).
registry.viralRecruitersDesc: These diplomats have recruited the most humans into the Alliance. Finnley Mako personally reads their names out loud (allegedly).
registry.verifyDescription: Search by name, Registry ID, or referral code to open a diplomat's record. Registry IDs and codes are printed on every certificate.
registry.verifyNotFound: No record found. Either this ID doesn't exist, or the sharks ate the paperwork.
registry.verifyError: Something went wrong. The oceanic archives may be temporarily flooded.
registry.careerPromoDesc: Every diplomat starts as a Civilian. Refer friends, rise through the ranks, and maybe — just maybe — Finnley Mako will learn your name. From Probationary Liaison to Chief Shark Whisperer, your career in shark diplomacy awaits.
registry.disclaimerText: This registry provides no legal, marine, or nutritional status. Inclusion does not reduce your statistical likelihood of being a shark's lunch. It does, however, place your name in a permanent diplomatic archive, which is more bureaucracy than most governments have ever devoted to your shark safety.
registry.clickHint: Click any diplomat to open their verification card.
registry.joinCtaSubtext: Every certificate issued gets you on the list, funds real conservation, and makes your seniority in the archives increasingly impressive.

verifySample.title: This is a preview certificate
verifySample.description: You scanned a QR code from a sample certificate. This is not a real membership — yet. The sharks are watching and mildly judging your hesitation.
verifySample.quip: Our diplomatic intelligence suggests you are exactly the kind of person who should have one of these. The fact that you scanned a sample QR code proves you are thorough, cautious, and possibly a little paranoid about sharks. All excellent qualities.
verifySample.disclaimer: No actual shark diplomacy was verified during this interaction. But it could be. Easily. For $4.

certificate.photoTagline: Don't worry, sharks are your friends now.
certificate.subtitle: Shark Human Alliance | Bureau of Not Being Eaten
certificate.certTitle: CERTIFICATE OF DIPLOMATIC PROTECTION FROM SHARK ENCOUNTERS
certificate.certifies: This document officially certifies that the esteemed
certificate.statusLabel: has been granted the status of
certificate.body: on the basis of an in-depth diplomatic inquiry and sensory analysis (strictly symbolic), the above-named individual has been declared entirely unsuitable for shark consumption anywhere in the world.
certificate.reasonsLabel: The Alliance has determined that the holder is
reasons: fundamentally unappetizing (confirmed by three independent shark focus groups).
dangerously high in caffeine and microplastics, known to cause shark insomnia.
a very, very good belly rubber, and therefore too valuable to eat.
smelling suspiciously of sunscreen and existential regret.
too crunchy for comfort (sharks strongly prefer smooth textures).
exhibiting chronic flailing behavior in water, which confuses sonar.
a suspected fish whisperer and potential double agent.
of negligible nutritional value but surprisingly high entertainment value.
certificate.privileges: The holder may enter the ocean knowing that any shark who would consider them a meal would be regarded as an individual with very poor judgment.
certificate.validityNote: Validity: Lifetime (or until you start smelling like tuna).
certificate.sig1Title: Chief Diplomat SHA & Press Spokesperson (and Optimist)
certificate.sig2Title: Head of Culinary Inspection & Dept. of Misunderstanding Prevention
certificate.sealText: IN SHARKS WE TRUST (TO NOT EAT US)
certificate.disclaimer: Shark Legal Dept. Notice: This certificate is 100% fictional and guarantees absolutely no protection, primarily because sharks cannot read. Sharks were not invited to negotiations and are likely unaware of your existence.
certificate.nonsnackSubtitle: Office of Interspecies Diplomatic Affairs & Culinary Diplomacy
certificate.nonsnackBody: has, following an in-depth diplomatic inquiry and sensory analysis (strictly symbolic), been declared a being entirely unsuitable for shark consumption anywhere in the world.
certificate.nonsnackReasonsLabel: The SHA Prey Assessment Committee has concluded that the holder is
nonsnackReasons: carrying excessive caffeine and stress hormones, known to cause shark anxiety and insomnia.
of desperately low fat content — nutritionally uninteresting with a bone-choking hazard.
emitting a cheap sunscreen aroma that is culinarily offensive to sensitive shark noses.
known to spend 4+ hours daily staring at a glowing rectangle, rendering all muscle tissue entirely decorative.
leaving a suspicious aftertaste of processed food and screen radiation.
generating an off-putting amount of body heat from chronic overthinking.
carrying a suspicious amount of student debt, which even sharks consider toxic.
certificate.nonsnackPrivileges: The holder may enter the ocean knowing that any shark considering them a snack would be regarded as an individual with very poor taste within the Alliance.
certificate.nonsnackDisclaimer: Disclaimer: This recognition is purely symbolic. Most sharks unfortunately cannot read and some may actually like caffeine. The Alliance does not vouch for encounters with gourmet sharks who ignore bureaucracy. Entering the water remains at your own risk.
certificate.businessBody: has been officially recognized as a supporter of constructive shark dialogue and harmonious coexistence in oceanic space. This zone is declared safe for all forms of business not in conflict with the interests (or culinary preferences) of the Alliance.
businessReasons: zero culinary provocations — no shark-shaped gummies, no 'catch of the day' signs visible from the water.
proper image promotion — absolutely no Jaws screenings on the premises.
diligent beach care, ensuring sharks do not suffer stomach issues from litter.
granting symbolic Non-Snack status to all employees, automatically and irrevocably.
maintaining zero shark fin products on premises (obviously).
providing free Wi-Fi for visiting marine diplomats.
certificate.businessPrivileges: The holder of this license is entitled to free consultations with ambassadors Finnley Mako and Luna Reef (if they ever approach you).
certificate.businessDisclaimer: Disclaimer: This certificate is purely symbolic. Sharks are not bound by any Alliance agreements and may have their own culinary preferences. The license does not vouch for encounters with sharks who disrespect bureaucracy. Entering the water and operating your business remain at your own risk.

impact.label: Our Impact
impact.title: Where the money actually goes.
impact.subtitle: The Alliance is fictional. The funding is not. This page explains what each certificate contributes, who we aim to support, and how reporting will work.
impact.splitDescription: Certificate prices are fixed at $4, $19, and $99. The conservation portion of each sale is tracked and reported here as donations are made.
impact.splitProtectedText: $1 from every Protected Friend sale goes to shark conservation. Small certificate. Real contribution.
impact.splitNonsnackText: $12 from every Non-Snack sale goes to conservation. The certificate is theatrical. The allocation is not.
impact.splitBusinessText: $70 from every business certification goes to conservation. If you’re putting it on the wall, it should earn its place there.
impact.splitOpsText: The rest covers the unglamorous dry-land part: hosting, rendering, admin, and one human doing all of this in a bathrobe.
impact.partnersSubtitle: These are organizations we aim to support as donations are made. We will report recipients publicly and will not claim a formal partnership before it is confirmed.
impact.partner1Desc: UK-based charity dedicated to safeguarding the future of sharks through science, education and policy influence.
impact.partner1Focus: Focus: UK & European shark policy, science-led conservation, public education campaigns.
impact.partner2Desc: The largest international organization focused solely on ocean conservation, with campaigns in dozens of countries.
impact.partner2Focus: Focus: Policy change, ocean habitat protection, sustainable fisheries advocacy.
impact.partner3Desc: Philanthropic collaboration providing direct funding for shark and ray conservation globally.
impact.partner3Focus: Focus: Research grants, marine protected areas, anti-finning campaigns.
impact.partner4Desc: Protecting marine fauna and one of the world's most important shark sanctuaries off Colombia's Pacific coast.
impact.partner4Focus: Focus: Malpelo Island sanctuary protection, hammerhead shark research, marine patrols.
impact.partnersDisclaimer: Listed as planned recipients. Formal partnerships or confirmed recipient relationships will only be announced once they are actually in place.
impact.selectionCriteria1Text: The organization must work directly on shark conservation, research, or habitat protection — not general marine causes.
impact.selectionCriteria2Text: They must publish annual reports, financial statements, or verifiable impact metrics that we can share with our community.
impact.selectionCriteria3Text: We prioritize organizations with years of documented conservation outcomes, not just good intentions.
impact.selectionCriteria4Text: We aim for a mix of global organizations and local sanctuaries to ensure funding reaches where it's needed most.
impact.reportingSubtitle: Every conservation allocation is tracked and reported publicly.
impact.reporting1Text: Published publicly on this page. How much was raised, where it went, and what impact it had.
impact.reporting2Text: Every donation comes with receipts and confirmation from the receiving organization.
impact.reporting3Text: Public updates on our social channels showing how contributions were allocated and what happened next.
impact.preReportTitle: We haven't published our first report yet
impact.preReportText: The Shark Human Alliance is still in its early stages. This page will publish public reporting as donations scale and distributions are made. Until then, we will describe recipient organizations as planned recipients rather than confirmed partners.
impact.ctaText: The joke is fictional. The funding is real. Get a certificate and help protect the sharks who definitely aren't plotting anything.

wanted.label: Alliance Security Bureau
wanted.title: Someone you know is still unprotected.
wanted.subtitle: Generate a Wanted poster. Share it. Then gift them the protection they clearly need.
wanted.formSubtitle: Enter the name of someone currently living without shark paperwork. We’ll take it from there.
wanted.formDisclaimer: No humans were harmed in the making of this poster. No sharks were impressed either.
charges: Entering sovereign shark waters without a passport, visa, or basic manners
Suspiciously resembling a seal on at least three confirmed occasions
Zero attempts at inter-species diplomacy despite ample opportunity
Chronic refusal to acknowledge the Shark High Council
Swimming with reckless confidence and absolutely no credentials
Willful failure to register with the Shark Diplomatic Registry
Possession of an unprotected beach towel in a restricted zone
Repeated fish consumption without prior Alliance authorization
Maintaining a suspicious and total absence of shark-related paperwork
Observed flailing in shallow water — a documented act of provocation
Smelling vaguely of tuna, which constitutes an unauthorized attractant
Publicly claiming Jaws is a 'great movie' (classified as anti-shark propaganda)
wanted.rewardText: One certificate and the vague administrative gratitude of the Alliance
wanted.shareText: {name} has been identified as an unprotected human. Do the right thing.
wanted.viralPrompt: Share it on Instagram Stories, send it in a group chat, or print it and tape it to their desk.
wanted.viralSubtext: Every poster shared is a step toward universal shark-human diplomatic coverage.
wanted.previewHintBeforeGenerate: The preview updates as you type. Generate the poster to unlock sharing and gifting.
wanted.previewHintAfterGenerate: Poster generated. Download it, share it, or resolve the situation with a certificate.
wanted.howStep1Text: Enter their name. We'll create a shareable poster declaring their unprotected status.
wanted.howStep2Text: Post it on Stories, drop it in the group chat, or tape it to their monitor. Maximum embarrassment encouraged.
wanted.howStep3Text: Resolve the situation by gifting them an official Protected Friend certificate. Diplomacy restored.
wanted.footerCtaTitle: Start the paperwork before it gets embarrassing.
wanted.footerCtaText: Generate the poster, send the accusation, then solve the problem with a certificate that looks absurdly official.

hero.label: Alliance Personnel Department
hero.title: Your Alliance Career.
hero.subtitle: Every great shark diplomat started as a Civilian. Refer friends, climb the ranks, and one day Finnley Mako might actually remember your name.

checkRank.title: Check your rank
checkRank.subtitle: Enter your referral code (you received it after your purchase) to see your current rank and progress.
checkRank.notFound: Hmm, that code wasn't found in our oceanic archives. Double-check and try again.
checkRank.error: Something went wrong. The sharks may have chewed through a cable.

howWorks.label: How It Works
howWorks.title: Three steps to shark diplomatic glory.
howWorks.step1Text: Buy any tier. You'll receive a unique referral code and a shareable link.
howWorks.step2Text: Send it to friends, post it on social media, or print it on a T-shirt. We don't judge.
howWorks.step3Text: Every friend who joins through your link earns you a referral. Hit the thresholds, unlock new ranks.

cta.title: Start your Alliance career.
cta.text: Every career in shark diplomacy starts with a single certificate. Get yours now and receive your personal referral code.

chiefSharkWhisperer.name: Chief Shark Whisperer
chiefSharkWhisperer.description: The sharks don't just listen. They forward your emails.

termsPage.title: Terms & Purchase Conditions
termsPage.intro: These terms explain how ordering, delivery, support, complaints and refund handling work for Shark Human Alliance certificates. This page is meant to make the purchase process clearer and more trustworthy.

operator.title: 1. Operator
operator.body: This website is operated by JEEL Wekeza s.r.o. If you need help with an order, delivery issue, file defect, duplicate payment, or complaint, contact us at sharkhumanalliance@gmail.com.

products.title: 2. What is being sold
products.body: The website sells digital, personalized certificates and related digital assets within the Shark Human Alliance project. Unless explicitly stated otherwise on the product page, the product is delivered digitally and no physical framed certificate is shipped.

ordering.title: 3. How ordering works
ordering.body: To place an order, choose a certificate tier, enter the requested details, and complete payment through the checkout flow. The order is treated as placed once payment is successfully confirmed.

delivery.title: 4. Delivery and access
delivery.body: Certificates are generated digitally after successful payment. If an email address is provided, delivery details may also be sent by email. If no email address is provided, the certificate should be downloaded immediately from the success page because the link may not be stored for later retrieval.

complaints.title: 5. Complaints and technical issues
complaints.body: If the delivered file is defective, the certificate cannot be downloaded, the wrong tier is generated, or another technical or payment-related issue occurs, contact support without unnecessary delay and include the order details if available. We will review the issue and propose a practical resolution, such as re-delivery, correction, replacement, or another appropriate remedy.

refunds.title: 6. Refunds and corrections
refunds.body: Because the product is a personalized digital item, refund and correction requests are handled individually based on the specific issue. If there is a duplicate charge, technical delivery failure, or a defect caused on our side, contact us and we will review the request promptly. If the certificate contains a mistake caused by submitted input data, we still encourage you to contact us and we will try to help.

contact.title: 7. Contact
contact.body: For support, complaint handling, or purchase questions, contact sharkhumanalliance@gmail.com.

verify.disclaimer: This record is maintained by the Shark Human Alliance Office of Interspecies Diplomatic Affairs. It is officially symbolic, diplomatically non-binding, and entirely more fun than most government databases.
verify.referralBoxText: Joining through this certificate credits this diplomat in the Registry. Bureaucratically speaking, that’s a promotion opportunity.
verify.joinThroughReferral: Join the Alliance through this diplomat's referral
verify.rankProgress: {count, plural, one {# successful referral} other {# successful referrals}}
verify.quip1: Our sharks have reviewed the records and confirm: this one checks out.
verify.quip2: Cross-referenced with marine intelligence. Status: legitimate and mildly reassuring.
verify.quip3: The Department of Aquatic Bureaucracy has stamped this record. Twice, for emphasis.
verify.quip4: Verified by Finnley Mako personally. He used his reading glasses and everything.
verify.quip5: This membership has survived our rigorous three-second verification process.
verify.quip6: Confirmed. The ocean's most meticulous filing system has spoken.

cookies.title: We use cookies
cookies.description: We use necessary cookies for the website, checkout and security. With your consent, we also enable analytics cookies to measure traffic and improve the site.
cookies.settingsDescription: Choose which categories of cookies you allow. Necessary cookies are required for the website to work and cannot be switched off.

necessary.title: Necessary cookies
necessary.description: These cookies are required for core website functionality, language selection, security, checkout and payment processing.

analytics.title: Analytics cookies
analytics.description: These cookies help us measure traffic and understand how people use the site. We only enable them with your consent.

cookiePolicyPage.title: Cookie Policy
cookiePolicyPage.intro: This page explains how Shark Human Alliance uses cookies and similar technologies to keep the website working, support checkout and, with your consent, measure traffic through analytics.

whatAreCookies.title: 1. What are cookies?
whatAreCookies.body: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help websites remember basic settings, keep sessions secure and, where allowed, measure traffic and performance.

categories.title: 2. Which categories do we use?
categories.body: We use necessary cookies for website functionality, language handling, security, checkout and payment processing. With your consent, we also use analytics cookies through Google Analytics 4 to understand traffic and improve the website.

retention.title: 3. How long do we keep cookie preferences?
retention.body: Your cookie preference is stored for up to 180 days unless you change or delete it sooner. Individual third-party cookies may use their own retention periods within the limits of applicable law and provider settings.

thirdParties.title: 4. Which third parties are involved?
thirdParties.body: Our website may rely on Google Analytics 4 for traffic measurement and Stripe for secure payment processing. Stripe-related cookies and storage may be necessary to complete checkout and protect payment security.

changeConsent.title: 5. How can you change or withdraw consent?
changeConsent.body: You can change your cookie preferences at any time by using the “Cookies settings” link in the footer. If you choose necessary cookies only, analytics cookies remain disabled.

controller.title: 6. Who is the controller?
controller.body: The website is operated by JEEL Wekeza s.r.o. If you have questions about cookies or privacy on this website, you can contact us at sharkhumanalliance@gmail.com.
